tax: Marl Walker is placed at species rank beneath Worked-Earthkin (Argillic order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a pale mixed clay-limestone body, crumbling edges that regrow, and a gritty texture unlike either pure clay or pure chalk. It is separated from its sibling the Loam Creeper by its pale stiff marl body and upright gait versus the Creeper's dark organics-rich slumping form; the two share the genus body-plan but never the organic-rich slump. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: calcium-rich clay marl at a clay-limestone contact seam, animated by earth-pressure.
ap: Marl Walker stands 1.4–1.8 m upright figure. Its silhouette is a rough humanoid outline of pale crumbling marl with an irregular surface. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a pale buff-grey colour with gritty white specks, unlike the dark grey of Pelitic clay kin; slow crumbling at limb-edges that re-consolidates within minutes from the contact layer; and a faint chalk-and-clay smell, half mineral, half earthy, unlike either pure type. It moves with a slow stiff walk on a broad base, leaving rounded imprints in soft floor, and shows none; pale buff-grey. Even after dispersal it can be identified from rounded foot-impressions in soft ground and a litter of pale gritty crumbles along its path, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Marl Walker persists in marl flats and clay-limestone contact zones where a mixed seam provides the worked-earth body its dual chemistry. It does not breed; it forms where earth-pressure at a clay-limestone contact seam animates the marl layer into a coherent walking body, and recurs whenever the contact seam replenishes from both layers after dispersal. It sustains its mass on earth-pressure at a dual-chemistry contact seam, drawing in marl drawn from the contact layer to repair and grow. Range is bounded by the clay-limestone contact zone: outside that band the body crumbles to loose grit away from its seam. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a marl flat where the contact layer has developed a rounded imprint-pattern too regular for water, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Marl Walker is roused by quarrying or heavy footfall near its marl flat and otherwise lies a pale crumbling mound indistinguishable from a marl hummock. It claims a single marl flat or contact-seam exposure and answers intrusion with advancing to push and bury, using its crumbling mass to obstruct and entomb. Toward grey-folk it is slow but obstructive; survey crews report that Forgemasters who chip marl exposures for flux find the hummocks that push back. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Marl Walker: a body of calcium-rich clay marl at a clay-limestone contact seam, animated by earth-pressure; immune to crushing, which only speeds the regrowth cycle; and a defining weakness — water saturation, which makes it too fluid to hold form, and sustained acid. Its senses run through the medium itself: earth-pressure sensing through the contact seam beneath it. It tolerates cold, damp, and darkness but fails at water saturation and acid dissolution. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the pale buff-grey body and crumbling-regrowth cycle, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Marl Walker are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it advances to entomb and obstruct with its bulk; it crumbles and reconsolidates to flow around barriers; it draws marl from the contact layer to repair any damage; and it can flatten to a low mound, posing as a natural feature. Each is a direct expression of earth-pressure in a mixed clay-limestone marl body, so it cannot, for instance, jet, conduct current, or cross water. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Marl Walker matters because it turns marl flats into slow entombing hazards, and Forgemasters now mark contact-seam hummocks before sampling. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an entombment hazard logged with clay-limestone contact zones, and its presence reshapes routes through the marl flats, now avoided as bedding and marked with contact-seam flags. A marl flat with regular rounded imprints that pre-date any crew passage marks a Walker's territory.
g: Marl Walker (Argilla calcarea) is a worked-earth elemental of lime-rich marl that forms at the interface of clay and limestone, walking as a pale, crumbling figure. Documented from the marl flats and clay-limestone contact zones of the mid tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a pale, gritty marl body that crumbles at the edges and regrows from the contact layer — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Marl is the mixed substance between clay and chalk; the Walker is likewise between the Pelitic clay kin and the chalk kin, sharing neither's extremes. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a slow collapse into pale gritty rubble that the seep can reconsolidate and no carcass.
